items.summary: The US government releases 160 previously classified files on alleged UFO sightings and alien life, including a photo of "unidentified phenomena" taken from the Moon's surface in 1969.

items.title: Tasmania rejected an on‑country hearing but other courts show it works
items.summary: An Aboriginal woman's request to have her Tasmanian court hearing held on country was rejected. But on-country hearings have been held in other jurisdictions. So how do they work?

items.title: Geraldton residents fear town will be the 'next Port Hedland' as mining dust complaints surge
items.summary: Geraldton residents say dust from expanding iron ore operations is blanketing homes, boats and businesses, fuelling fears the coastal city could turn into "the next Port Hedland".

items.title: Former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay allegedly groomed teenage boy with dinners, alcohol
items.summary: Court documents reveal allegations former ACT attorney-general Gordon Ramsay, who is charged with grooming a boy, allegedly set up an account on the cloud for the teen to share photos in increasing states of undress.

items.title: If a week is a long time in politics, Julie Bishop's resignation as ANU chancellor shows six months is a lifetime at a university
items.summary: December 31 was the date when Julie Bishop was due to finish her term as Australian National University chancellor, so why — after having repeatedly stated she would see her commitment through — did she leave months early?

items.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 hopes you're as distracted as you are nostalgic
items.summary: Miranda Priestly has lost her fangs in the reboot as weak plot lines make room for superfluous scenes and characters.

items.title: Concussion treatment an evolving area with a long road to recovery
items.summary: Liam Dennis was at the top of his game when, in 2023, a crushing blow to the head brought his football career to a halt.

items.title: Chris Minns staffer's legal win 'chipping away' at democracy
items.summary: The NSW premier faced an extraordinary question this week amid a deteriorating relationship between his government and the Legislative Council.

items.title: Australia is breaking its carbon bond, but there's a cost
items.summary: As Australia weans itself away from coal, aging power stations across the country have been reduced to smoke and rubble. But for communities at the coalface, there are serious and dramatic consequences.

items.title: Alex De Minaur destroys racquet in Italian Open exit as Novak Djokovic also out
items.summary: Struggling Alex de Minaur suffers another early clay-court tournament exit, this time at the Italian Open in Rome.

items.title: Victoria's treaty leaders have been elected. Here's how the new structure will work
items.summary: The new leaders of Victoria's First Peoples' Assembly say they're pushing ahead with promises made under the state's treaty, despite political uncertainty.

items.title: Immigration provides tricky policy tightrope in migrant-reliant Farrer
items.summary: Many Farrer industries rely heavily on migration, while some communities face housing supply issues, making immigration a challenging topic for candidates.

items.title: Royal commission exposes depths of antisemitism in Australia after first week of public hearings
items.summary: Fear, concerns for safety and the impact on children dominated the first week of hearings of the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion.

items.title: David Attenborough at 100 'warm, well-informed and delightful company'
items.summary: His legendary environmental documentaries have captivated generations, but away from the screen, Sir David Attenborough is "warm, well-informed and delightful company", too.

items.title: Family of ousted Myanmar leader Aung San Suu Kyi call for 'proof of life'
items.summary: Aung San Suu Kyi's son demands proof his mother is still alive, as Myanmar's military junta keeps tens of thousands of political prisoners behind bars.

items.title: Deaf and blind cricketers feel disrespected by changes to Cricket Australia's funding
items.summary: Significant changes to how Cricket Australia funds Deaf, blind and intellectually disabled teams prompt concern from players and advocates.

items.title: Mother's Day complicated and full of love for kids in foster care
items.summary: Jaharn grew up in foster care from two years of age. Now 19, he and his foster mum share their lessons of love.

items.title: Australians with motor neurone disease fear losing priority pathway under NDIS reforms
items.summary: Australians with MND who have rapidly changing needs fear they will lose a priority pathway to access the National Disability Insurance Scheme under Labor’s planned changes.

items.title: Military parades and promises of peace ring hollow when words lose their meaning
items.summary: Displays of military strength and perpetual promises of peace do little to mask the reality that major powers aren't always able to control the wars they helped create.

items.title: Voters in Farrer welcome by-election day after protracted campaign
items.summary: After three months of intense campaigning, voters in the NSW electorate say whoever wins today's poll must act on the promise to make noise in Canberra.

items.title: Billions in investment waiting for biofuel policy certainty, super investors say
items.summary: Billions of dollars worth of investment will flow if the federal government mandates a percentage of biofuels be added to the nation's fuel supply, major investors say.

items.title: Some people will hate Tuesday's federal budget but Jim Chalmers doesn't mind
items.summary: For once, the budget won't be a dull one. Jim Chalmers's tax reforms will create a stir and some question whether they will close the generation gap, but the treasurer is projecting a relaxed figure.

items.title: Today in History, May 9: The 'magnificent' opening of Australia's first federal parliament
items.summary: More than 12,000 people packed into Melbourne's Royal Exhibition Building to witness the "pomp and ceremony" of a "magnificent" occasion in the country's history.

items.title: Australian author Ellena Savage considers our ecological disinheritance in debut novel, The Ruiners
items.summary: Ellena Savage's debut essay collection, Blueberries, was a surprise hit. In her new novel, The Ruiners, she takes on Charles Dickens and the illegal waste trade.

items.title: Budget night productivity package to include migration overhaul and red tape drive
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says Australia's productivity problem will take a long time to fix, but a $10 billion annual saving on business compliance costs is a start.

items.title: Japan confirms first fatal bear attack this year with two more suspected
items.summary: Japanese authorities say a 55-year-old woman found dead last month was killed by a bear. It is the first confirmed fatal bear attack for 2026 following a record 13 deaths last year.

items.title: 'I don't coach for my job': Voss defiant after latest loss, Dogs edge Port
items.summary: Carlton coach Michael Voss is frustrated after a close loss to Brisbane, but denies he is coaching for his job, while the Western Bulldogs hold off Port Adelaide despite being held to one goal in the second half.

items.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Hobart man fined for performing Nazi salute in front of police outside waterfront bar
items.summary: The man pleaded guilty to four charges, with a magistrate describing the Nazi gesture charge as a very serious offence.

items.title: 'We don't want the penalty': Moses wins the game but doesn't end it
items.summary: Mitchell Moses stuns the Cowboys in a bizarre golden-point finish, after Parramatta crosses twice in the final six minutes of normal time.

items.title: Investigation into child abuse network ends with 62 arrests in NSW
items.summary: A probe into a cloud-based image-sharing folder resulted in the largest amount of child abuse material files seized in a single investigation in NSW history.

items.title: NT taxpayers on the hook for $75m if Beetaloo Basin fracking project defaults
items.summary: Northern Territory taxpayers will be hit with a $75 million bill if Tamboran Resources, one of the key Beetaloo Basin fracking companies, is unable to repay its lenders.

items.title: Bundaberg man sentenced to 10 years' jail for fatal hit-and-run crash while 'drag racing''
items.summary: Lachlan Robert Carver has been sentenced to 10 years for a hit-and-run crash that killed cyclist Matthew Nilsen.

items.title: Chinese oil tanker's crew raises safety concerns after attack near Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: The chief engineer of a Chinese-owned oil tanker attacked near the Strait of Hormuz says it is "still unclear" who fired on the vessel.

items.title: Closing statements heard in Charlotte MacInnes's defamation case against Rebel Wilson
items.summary: Final arguments in the defamation trial between Charlotte MacInnes and Rebel Wilson have heard claims of a suspicious career jump to "terrible, terrible" allegations.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's father Raphael Granites to remain behind bars on domestic violence charges
items.summary: The father of Kumanjayi Little Baby will remain in prison for aggravated assault, despite calls from his lawyers for his release to attend cultural grieving for his daughter.

items.title: Inquest told Queensland prisoner Glen Francis died weeks after pain dismissed as ‘drug-seeking behaviour’
items.summary: A Queensland prisoner died in prison of a rare bacterial condition after his calls for medical help went unanswered for weeks, a medical expert has told a coronial inquest.

items.title: Why there's no end in sight for Kezie Apps, Origin's ageless wonder
items.summary: Kezie Apps will be the oldest State of Origin player across both states and the men's and women's series this year, but the 35-year-old Blues stalwart shows no signs of slowing down.

items.title: Bega's costs are up 10pc and customers will pay 'a large chunk' of it
items.summary: One of Australia's biggest dairy manufacturers, Bega, is warning about soaring food inflation, as the war in the Middle East disrupts global supply chains.

items.title: Sports Minister Anika Wells repays $10,000 in travel costs after audit
items.summary: An audit of Communications and Sports Minister Anika Wells's travel by the Independent Parliamentary Expenses Authority found four breaches, but cleared her controversial trips to New York and Thredbo.

items.title: NSW Police appealing for help to solve 'suspicious' December 2025 gunshot death in Temora
items.summary: Police believe CCTV footage shows the final movements of the 18-year-old before he was found dead near a culvert.

items.title: Police appeal for information following suspicious death in Temora
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Police advised to disable bluetooth on Axon body-worn cameras to avoid detection by criminals
items.summary: Thousands of police officers have been advised to disable the bluetooth on their Axon body-worn cameras during raids and undercover jobs to avoid being detected and tracked, after Four Corners exposed a security flaw with the devices.

items.title: Former Sydney Knox Grammar teacher spared jail time after procuring child for sexual activity
items.summary: William Roberto Gulson was on Friday spared time behind bars but will be placed on the NSW Child Protection Register for eight years.

items.title: Trio guilty of murdering Newcastle man Anthony Nugent over fake gun deal
items.summary: Jury finds trio guilty of murder over the shooting death of Newcastle man Anthony Nugent over a fake gun deal in 2022.

items.title: Man jailed over axe rampage and apartment block fire in Sarina
items.summary: A man who burnt down an apartment block and terrorised a central Queensland town with an axe is jailed for six years, but will be eligible for parole in February with time already served in custody.

items.title: Charges dropped against AFL players Dion Prestia and Steven May over alleged brawl
items.summary: Prosecutors have dropped all charges against current and former footballers Dion Prestia and Steven May over an alleged brawl in Sorrento on Victoria's Mornington Peninsula on Boxing Day in 2024.

items.title: Richard Lewer wins Archibald Prize 2026 for portrait of Pitjantjatjara elder Iluwanti Ken
items.summary: The Melbourne-based artist painted Pitjantjatjara elder Iluwanti Ken, who is known for her large-scale drawings and was also a finalist in this year's Wynne Prize.

items.title: Jaeden Tito and Rabii Zahabe found guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston
items.summary: A jury has found Jaeden Tito and Rabii Zahabe guilty of murdering underworld figure Gavin Preston outside a Melbourne cafe in 2023.

items.title: Battle for Farrer sees major parties on the outer as voters switch loyalties
items.summary: The Coalition has held the seat of Farrer for more than 70 years, but voters sense that could change after Saturday's by-election.

items.title: Woman charged with murder of Wayne Pedro Morseu
items.summary: A 46-year-old woman has been charged with the murder and interfering with the corpse of Wayne Pedro Morseu in north Queensland.

items.title: Shabtay Yaacoby found guilty of laundering more than $10m in landmark investment scam trial
items.summary: Israeli citizen Shabtay Yaacoby is found guilty of laundering more than $10 million stolen by investment scammers.

items.title: Jewish teacher feared for his life marching in Sydney Mardi Gras parade, royal commission hears
items.summary: The man told the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion he was embraced by the LGBTQIA+ community when he came out as gay as a teenager, but the experience was markedly different when he told loved ones of his religious conversion.

items.title: E-bike rider dies after crash with orange bus in Parmelia in Perth's south
items.summary: A 37-year-old man has died after the electric bicycle he was riding hit a bus in Parmelia on Thursday afternoon.

items.title: SA government prepped to chop down more than 1,000 trees for Adelaide Parklands projects
items.summary: More than 1,000 trees are scheduled to be chopped down in Adelaide's Parklands if all the South Australian government's projects go ahead as planned.

items.title: Boral withdraws Reedy Creek quarry proposal after months of community opposition
items.summary: After months of protests and community backlash, Boral has officially withdrawn its development application for the proposed Reedy Creek quarry. Local residents and campaigners are celebrating the major turnaround

items.title: Statement from mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby read at Alice Springs vigil, as nation mourns five-year-old girl
items.summary: At a vigil held in Alice Springs last night for Kumanjayi Little Baby, a statement was read on behalf of her mother who called for the community to remember her five-year-old child as a "pretty girl in pink" who was "truly loved" by her family.

items.title: Cockatoo removed from Orange's Cook Park aviary amid welfare concerns
items.summary: Orange City Council says it is meeting all the requirements of its licence to operate the Cook Park aviary but concerned locals say it is time the enclosure was closed for good.

items.title: Magnetic South land clearing found not to have breached national environmental law
items.summary: The federal government says Magnetic South's land clearing last year was not illegal, but a conservation group says FOI documents raise questions over the validity of the decision.

items.title: Joint NSW–Victoria police operation targets outlaw motorcycle gangs and organised crime
items.summary: Police from Victoria and NSW spend a week on the Victoria–NSW border targeting outlaw motorcycle gangs and organised crime.

items.title: United States trade court rules against Trump's 10 per cent tariff rate
items.summary: A United States trade court rules against President Donald Trump's universal 10 per cent tariff rate, dealing another blow to his signature economic policy.

items.title: Do you have a mother wound? Here's how to heal it
items.summary: It's the yearning ache that comes from reckoning with the imperfect human mother we get, against the idealised fantasy version we wish for.

items.title: Queensland Audit Office finds poor bookkeeping responsible for billion dollars in 'found assets'
items.summary: A Queensland Audit Office report finds a $1.34 billion discrepancy between what appeared on council books and maps.

items.title: NBA Playoffs: LeBron and Embiid's resurgence and Knicks title shot among five first-round lessons
items.summary: LeBron James shows you can never count out a team he is on, even at 41, as the first round of the playoffs wraps up.

items.title: Real Madrid dressing room spat sees Federico Valverde taken to hospital after clash with Aurélien Tchouaméni
items.summary: Teammates Federico Valverde and Aurélien Tchouaméni are involved in an altercation at Real Madrid training that left skipper Valverde in hospital with a head trauma.
